summary: The Docker-based reproduction successfully demonstrates that Poetry's extractall function allows path traversal in tar archives, enabling arbitrary file writes outside the intended extraction directory. A malicious package with path traversal sequences in file paths can write files to arbitrary locations on the victim's system during package installation.
expected_security_impact: The malicious tarball should write files outside the extraction directory to /tmp/poetry_path_traversal_proof.txt and /tmp/pwned.txt, demonstrating arbitrary file write capability through path traversal in tarfile extraction.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh built the Docker image with Python 3.10 (vulnerable version), created a malicious tarball with path traversal sequences, extracted it using Poetry's extractall function, and successfully wrote files to /tmp/poetry_path_traversal_proof.txt outside the extraction directory, confirming the vulnerability.
verification_method: The verify.sh script checks for the existence of files written outside the extraction directory (/tmp/poetry_path_traversal_proof.txt and /tmp/pwned.txt). If these files exist, it confirms the path traversal succeeded. The test uses Python 3.10 which is within the vulnerable version range and demonstrates the exploit working as expected.
evidence_produced_by_run: Malicious tarball created with path traversal sequences (../../../tmp/pwned.txt and ../../../tmp/poetry_path_traversal_proof.txt)
Poetry's extractall function called on the malicious tarball
Files successfully written to /tmp/poetry_path_traversal_proof.txt outside the extraction directory
Content of proof file matches expected malicious content
Python version 3.10 confirmed vulnerable to tarfile path traversal
limitations_or_blockers: Vulnerability only affects specific Python versions: < 3.8.17, 3.9.0-3.9.16, 3.10.0-3.10.11, 3.11.0-3.11.3, and broken tarfile.data_filter versions 3.9.17, 3.10.12, 3.11.4
Newer Python versions (3.8.17+, 3.9.18+, 3.10.13+, 3.11.5+) have tarfile.data_filter working correctly
ZIP files (including wheels) remain vulnerable on ALL Python versions as they have no validation in helpers.py:414

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable commit, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
